subject: Computer Networking
title: HTTPS and TLS
description: A quick win: understand HTTPS and TLS.

hook.text: The 'S' in HTTPS means your connection is encrypted. Without it, anyone on the network can read your data.

buildup.text: TLS wraps HTTP in encryption. The server proves its identity with a certificate signed by a trusted authority.

discovery.text: TLS 1.3 completes the handshake in one round trip. Faster than TLS 1.2, which needed two round trips.

twist.text: Let's Encrypt made certificates free and automated. HTTPS went from expensive to default in five years.

climax.text: Browsers now mark HTTP sites as 'Not Secure.' Over 95% of Chrome page loads use HTTPS today.

punchline.text: HTTPS encrypts the web. Free certificates made it universal.

quiz.question: How did Let's Encrypt change HTTPS adoption?
options: It made TLS certificates free and automated
It created a faster version of the TLS protocol
It blocked all HTTP traffic on the internet
